seo.title: No-bake cheesecake - pretty, delicious and quick - Marinda Kook

html:
Cheesecake remains a winner. This cheesecake is so easy and it tastes wonderful. It’s not too sweet and the berries on top give it a refreshing flavour. People are scared to work with gelatine. Believe me, if I can do it, so can you. It’s play-easy simple.

 

YOU NEED:

  • 1 packet Romany Creams
  • 60 ml melted butter
  • 3 x tubs smooth cream cheese
  • 1 tin condensed milk
  • 5 ml vanilla extract
  • 2 tubs granadilla pulp
  • 60 ml lemon juice
  • 15 g gelatine (one and a half sachets)
  • 90 ml water

TOPPING

  • 1 packet frozen berries
  • 100 ml sugar
  • 5 ml gelatine

NOW:

  1. crush the biscuits and mix them with the melted butter
  2. spray a springform pan with Spray and Cook and press the crumbs firmly onto the base (put it in the fridge for now)
  3. mix the cream cheese, condensed milk, vanilla essence, lemon juice and granadilla pulp until smooth
  4. dissolve the gelatine in the water (a few seconds in the microwave)
  5. mix the gelatine mixture into the cream cheese
  6. pour it over the crumbs and put it in the fridge

TOPPING

  1. simmer the berries and sugar until the sugar has dissolved
  2. take it off the stove and stir in the gelatine
  3. let it cool a little and pour it over the cream cheese mixture that's in the fridge
  4. place the cheesecake back in the fridge
  5. leave it in the fridge overnight and show off your beautiful cheesecake tomorrow

The cheesecake not only looks pretty with the blood-red berries, but tastes wonderful too.
